heading: Disable LDAP Configuration
body:
**Atlascli:**Atlas CLI> **Note:** You can use the same Atlas CLI command to disable LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol)
authentication settings and LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization settings.To delete one LDAP configuration using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas security ldap delete [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas security ldap delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-security-ldap-delete).**Ui:**Atlas UITo disable your current LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) settings using the Atlas UI:Atlas disables your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authentication settings and your
LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization settings.To disable LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization only, toggle the button next to LDAP Authorization to OFF. Atlas disables your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization settings.
